get.summary: Ophalen van een registratiebewijs van een voertuig.

parameters.name: overtreding
parameters.description: Soort overtreding waarvoor het registratiebewijs wordt opgevraagd.

parameters.name: land
parameters.description: Het land waarvan de data wordt aangevraagd.

parameters.name: kenteken1
parameters.description: Het eerste deel van het kenteken.

parameters.name: kenteken2
parameters.description: Het tweede deel van het kenteken.

parameters.name: datum
parameters.description: De datum waarop de overtreding werd vastgesteld. Indien niet opgegeven wordt de huidige datum en tijd gebruikt.

parameters.name: embed
parameters.description: De data die moet worden opgehaald, kan worden beperkt tot een bepaalde set zoals enkel het voertuig of bestuurder.

Registratiebewijs.bestuurder.description: De waarde van het attribuut `@id` kan gebruikt worden om het gerefereerde object van het type `Agent` op te halen.

Registratiebewijs.eigenaar.description: De waarde van het attribuut `@id` kan gebruikt worden om het gerefereerde object van het type `Agent` op te halen.

Registratiebewijs.voertuig.description: De waarde van het attribuut `@id` kan gebruikt worden om het gerefereerde object van het type `Voertuig` op te halen.

HdrAuthorization.name: Authorization
HdrAuthorization.description: The **Authorization** header is used to provide the access token for the request. The access token is a bearer token that is obtained from the authorization server after successful authentication and authorization. It is used to authenticate the client and authorize access to the requested resource.

HdrX-Correlation-Id.name: X-Correlation-Id
HdrX-Correlation-Id.description: The **X-Correlation-ID** is a free identifier created by the client to identify a request and all related requests in the context of a single transaction. It is used by the service to correlate requests and responses across multiple services and components, e.g. for logging and debugging purposes. It is recommended to use a UUID version 4 (random) string with enough entropy to avoid collisions.

HdrLegalPurpose.name: Legal-Purpose
HdrLegalPurpose.description: A unique identifier indicating the legal purpose. When no unique identifier received from Mobility it can be left empty. Should especially be used in combination with a 'cumul-clientID'.

HdrOrganisation.name: Organisation
HdrOrganisation.description: Identification of the organisation technically sending the request to SPF Mobility&Transport. It is the official 10-digit company number as known in the official company register.

HdrTarget-Organisation.name: Target-Organisation
HdrTarget-Organisation.description: Identification of the organisation for which the information is intended, from which the information is coming or which is business-wise responsible. It is the official 10-digit company number as known in the official company register.

HdrUser-Organisation.name: User-Organisation
HdrUser-Organisation.description: Identification of the organisation of the user.

HdrUser-Organisation-Description.name: User-Organisation-Description
HdrUser-Organisation-Description.description: Description of the organisation of the user.

HdrApplication.name: Application
HdrApplication.description: The requesting business application, agreed and predefined by SPF Mobility&Transports and is based on the business authorisation to the target-organisation.

HdrUser-Id.name: User-Id
HdrUser-Id.description: Identification of the person who is executing the request; the identification needs to be done by the users own organisation. It can be a reference which allows the organisation to retrieve the user within the organisation, the organisation can use the legal logging of transactions where a link between this reference and the user is logged. It cannot be the 11-digit national number of the user. This header is kept in the loggings and when necessary (for example in case of a legal investigation) the organisation needs to be able to identify the user.

HdrUser-Reference.name: User-Reference
HdrUser-Reference.description: Identification of the reference assigned by the user.

HdrAccept-Language.name: Accept-Language
HdrAccept-Language.description: Language in which valued properties of the response should be returned.
